summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex review findings on PR #669 in fogodev/ars-ui by fixing four combobox edge cases, then shepherd the PR through CI and back into the Codex review loop.
summary.outcome: All four Codex-flagged issues were fixed with regression tests, CI passed (after a runner disk-space failure was resolved by rerunning), and the commit `fix(components): address combobox codex edge cases` was pushed. A fresh `@codex review` request was posted and the session ended awaiting new Codex feedback.

repo: Codex review state is tracked via reactions on the PR issue itself (not on the trigger comment); the signal to check is a 👀 or 👍 reaction from the codex user on the issue.
The project uses `cargo nextest` for unit tests and `cargo llvm-cov` for coverage; both are required gates before merge.
Boolean attributes are serialized as the string `"true"` (via `set_bool`), not as a bare boolean — test assertions must match this format.
The repo has a `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` skill file that defines the exact loop protocol for handling Codex review cycles.
The combobox machine distinguishes passive `UpdateItems` opens from user-initiated opens; merging them causes the list to open unintentionally on controlled refreshes.
The `Set::All` / `__ars_all` sentinel is reserved for internal custom-commit tracking and must not be selectable as a real option.
Controlled selection bindables must be updated when item pruning removes a currently-selected value, not just the local state.

code.finding: Controlled selection pruning path — needed to also notify bindable consumers when pruning changes the active selection value.
UpdateItems handler — passive refresh was incorrectly reusing the user-input open path, causing the dropdown to open on data refreshes.
Custom key / hidden-input sentinel serialization — the `__ars_all` / `Set::All` value must remain reserved and not surface as a selectable item.
Clear-trigger button attrs — readonly state must disable the clear button the same way disabled state does; initial patch accidentally hit the adjacent trigger branch instead.

workflow: GitHub runner `No space left on device` errors are infra failures, not code failures — check job annotations before debugging test output, and use `gh run rerun --failed` rather than re-pushing.
The GraphQL `reviewThreads` query needs balanced braces; an extra `}` causes silent query failures that return no results, making it appear there are zero unresolved threads.
Poll Codex review state via three parallel checks: `gh pr checks`, PR-level reactions, and unresolved thread count — running them concurrently with `sleep` reduces round-trip latency.
Write focused regression tests before touching implementation paths so pre-fix failures confirm test validity before fixes are applied.
Run `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, the targeted test slice, and `git diff --check` as a local gate before committing review fixes.

friction: The GraphQL query had a repeated brace typo (`} }` at the end) that caused multiple silent failures, requiring several re-attempts before the correct query was identified.
The CI `Unit Tests` job failed with `No space left on device` — a GitHub runner infra issue unrelated to the patch — consuming significant polling time before the root cause was diagnosed.
Codex's review reaction/signal mechanism was initially misread as being on the trigger comment rather than on the PR issue itself, causing wasted polling.
The clear-trigger readonly fix initially patched the wrong branch (adjacent trigger path instead of the clear button attrs), requiring a corrective patch.
The `_rerun_failed_workflow_run_jobs` approach required multiple 150–240 second sleep-poll cycles before unit tests, adapter tests, coverage, and release verification all completed.
open_items: Awaiting new Codex review pass on commit `664d73a7` / the follow-up fix commit — the session ended before Codex produced a 👀 reaction or new threads.
Coverage and release-verification jobs were the last to complete; their specific numbers were not captured — worth checking that coverage did not regress.

The `@codex review` comment trigger mechanism is fragile (reaction-based polling with no webhook); consider whether the `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` loop should be formalized as a scheduled agent.
